summarization.a: The figure shows the precursor coverage (Precursor cov.) against axial position (m) for different values of $\beta_{bp}$.
summarization.b: The figure shows the byproduct coverage (Byproduct cov.) against axial position (m) for different values of $\beta_{bp}$.
summarization.c: The figure shows the precursor coverage against the axial position for different values of precursor partial pressure.

b.question: How does increasing the precursor pressure influence by-product adsorption for β_bp0 = 10⁻³?
b.answer: Increasing the precursor pressure reduces the impact of by-product adsorption, leading to weaker axial coverage gradients.

a.question: What was the precursor partial pressure?
What precursor reaction probability is assumed for all simulations shown in this figure?
How does increasing the reaction probability of the by-product affect the precursor surface coverage under saturated dosing conditions?
a.answer: Increasing the by-product reaction probability introduces progressively stronger axial gradients in the precursor surface coverage, even though the process is saturated, because adsorbed by-products occupy surface sites and reduce the available precursor coverage according to Θ = 1 − Θ_bp.
